sections.subtitle: Recent Form Analysis
sections.text: KVC Westerlo has shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, scoring 10 goals but conceding heavily. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, on the other hand, has struggled with only 2 wins in their last 5, scoring just 4 goals while conceding 5. Westerlo appears to have a slight edge in attacking form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Westerlo has been dominant with a 2-0 win and a 2-2 draw against Leuven. Historically, Westerlo has not lost to Leuven in the last 6 encounters, giving them a psychological advantage.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Overview
sections.text: Westerlo ranks higher in goal scoring (16 goals, 3rd in league) despite poor defensive stats (20 conceded, last in league). Leuven struggles offensively (8 goals, near bottom) and defensively (17 conceded), indicating a weaker overall performance compared to Westerlo.

sections.subtitle: Key Metrics for Prediction
sections.text: Corners per game average around 5 for both teams, and head-to-head matches often see over 10.5 corners. Yellow cards trend higher for Leuven recently (average over 4 per game), and the referee’s average of 4 per game supports this. Goals are expected given Westerlo’s attacking strength at home.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, KVC Westerlo leads Oud-Heverlee Leuven by a score of 1-0 at Het Kuipje. The home side took the lead in the 19th minute through a well-taken goal by Nacho Ferri, and they've managed to hold onto that advantage. Westerlo has shown more control in the game, dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third and penalty area. Meanwhile, Leuven has had their moments but struggled to convert their chances into goals, missing a big opportunity in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KVC Westerlo has been the more dominant side in terms of ball possession and territorial control. They've created a big chance and converted it through Ferri, while their defensive structure has limited Leuven's effectiveness in the final third. Their high percentage of duels won, particularly in the air, indicates physical superiority over their opponents. On the other hand, Oud-Heverlee Leuven has shown flashes of attacking intent but lacks the cutting edge. Despite creating a big chance, their inability to win duels and gain possession in dangerous areas has hindered their progress. Their defensive frailties, especially in aerial battles, have been exposed by Westerlo's approach.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Westerlo, Nacho Ferri has been the standout performer, not only for his goal but also for his involvement in the attacking plays. His positioning and composure have been crucial. Arthur Piedfort has also played a pivotal role with key passes that have unlocked Leuven's defense at times. On the Leuven side, Casper Terho has been their most dangerous player, coming close to scoring but missing a significant chance. Takuma Ominami's contribution in creating opportunities has been notable, though the team's overall lack of cohesion has limited his impact.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw several shifts in momentum between the two sides. Initially, Leuven started with intent, pushing forward with purpose in the opening minutes. However, Westerlo quickly took control, building sustained attacking spells around the time of their goal and maintaining pressure in the following minutes. Leuven responded with a few bursts of attacking play midway through the half, but they couldn't sustain the push. Towards the end of the half, Westerlo regained dominance with a series of attacking waves, keeping Leuven on the back foot until the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, KVC Westerlo will likely aim to maintain their control and exploit their physical advantage to create more scoring opportunities. They may look to Nacho Ferri once again to lead their attack. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, trailing by a goal, will need to take more risks and push for an equalizer, which could leave them vulnerable at the back. If they can improve their duel-winning rate and capitalize on their chances, they might turn the game around. However, Westerlo's current momentum suggests they are more likely to extend their lead than concede.
